questions.question: Who directed the movie 'Jaws'?
questions.explanation: Spielberg directed Jaws in 1975.

questions.question: What band released 'Bohemian Rhapsody'?
questions.explanation: Queen released it in 1975 on 'A Night at the Opera.'

questions.question: What is the highest-grossing film of all time (unadjusted)?
questions.explanation: Avatar (2009) earned over $2.9 billion worldwide.

questions.question: In which TV show would you find Walter White?
questions.explanation: Bryan Cranston played Walter White (2008-2013).

questions.question: Who wrote the novel '1984'?
questions.explanation: Orwell published 1984 in 1949.

questions.question: What video game franchise features Master Chief?
questions.explanation: Master Chief is the protagonist of the Halo series.

questions.question: Who composed 'The Four Seasons'?
questions.explanation: Vivaldi composed it around 1720.

questions.question: What was the first feature-length animated film?
questions.explanation: El Apóstol was an Argentine political satire; no copies survive.

questions.question: Which author created Discworld?
questions.explanation: Pratchett wrote 41 Discworld novels (1983-2015).

questions.question: What instrument does a pianist play?
questions.explanation: The piano was invented around 1700.

questions.question: What franchise features a character named Darth Vader?
questions.explanation: Darth Vader first appeared in Star Wars (1977).

questions.question: Who played Jack in the movie Titanic?
questions.explanation: DiCaprio starred opposite Kate Winslet in the 1997 film.

questions.question: What is the name of Harry Potter's best friend (male)?
questions.explanation: Ron and Harry met on the Hogwarts Express.

questions.question: Who sang 'Thriller'?
questions.explanation: Thriller (1982) is the best-selling album of all time.

questions.question: What animated movie features a clownfish named Nemo?
questions.explanation: Finding Nemo was released by Pixar in 2003.

questions.question: Who created the TV series 'The Simpsons'?
questions.explanation: The Simpsons debuted in 1989 and is the longest-running animated sitcom.

questions.question: What band is Thom Yorke the lead singer of?
questions.explanation: Radiohead formed in 1985 in Oxford, England.

questions.question: Who directed 'Pulp Fiction'?
questions.explanation: Pulp Fiction was released in 1994 and won the Palme d'Or.

questions.question: What is the best-selling video game of all time?
questions.explanation: Minecraft has sold over 300 million copies.

questions.question: Who wrote 'Pride and Prejudice'?
questions.explanation: Austen published it in 1813.

questions.question: What movie features the quote 'Here's looking at you, kid'?
questions.explanation: Humphrey Bogart says the line in the 1942 classic.

questions.question: Who painted 'Starry Night'?
questions.explanation: Van Gogh painted it in 1889 while in an asylum.

questions.question: What TV series is set in the fictional land of Westeros?
questions.explanation: Game of Thrones aired from 2011 to 2019 on HBO.

questions.question: Who is the author of 'The Lord of the Rings'?
questions.explanation: Tolkien published the trilogy between 1954 and 1955.

questions.question: What instrument has 88 keys?
questions.explanation: A standard piano has 52 white keys and 36 black keys.

questions.question: What film won the first Academy Award for Best Picture?
incorrectAnswers: All Quiet on the Western Front (1930)
questions.explanation: Wings, a silent WWI film, won at the first ceremony in 1929.

questions.question: Who wrote 'One Hundred Years of Solitude'?
questions.explanation: Márquez published it in 1967; it's a pillar of magical realism.

questions.question: What was the first commercially successful video game?
questions.explanation: Pong was released by Atari in 1972.

questions.question: Who composed 'The Rite of Spring'?
questions.explanation: Its 1913 premiere famously caused a riot in Paris.

questions.question: What Japanese filmmaker directed 'Seven Samurai'?
questions.explanation: Kurosawa released Seven Samurai in 1954.

questions.question: What Disney movie features a glass slipper?
questions.explanation: Cinderella was Disney's 12th animated feature, released in 1950.

questions.question: Who played Iron Man in the Marvel Cinematic Universe?
questions.explanation: Downey first played Iron Man in 2008.

questions.question: What genre is 'The Shining'?
questions.explanation: Stanley Kubrick directed this 1980 horror classic.

questions.question: What Netflix series follows chess prodigy Beth Harmon?
questions.explanation: The 2020 miniseries sparked a chess boom worldwide.

questions.question: Who wrote 'The Great Gatsby'?
questions.explanation: Fitzgerald published it in 1925.

questions.question: What is the longest-running Broadway musical?
questions.explanation: Phantom ran from 1988 to 2023 on Broadway.

questions.question: What author wrote 'Beloved'?
questions.explanation: Morrison won the Pulitzer Prize for Beloved in 1988.

questions.question: What film technique is Alfred Hitchcock known for?
questions.explanation: Hitchcock was called the 'Master of Suspense.'

questions.question: What is the name of Batman's butler?
questions.explanation: Alfred Pennyworth has served the Wayne family for decades.

questions.question: What Pixar movie features emotions as characters?
questions.explanation: Inside Out (2015) personifies Joy, Sadness, Anger, Fear, and Disgust.

questions.question: Who directed 'Inception'?
questions.explanation: Nolan wrote and directed Inception (2010).

questions.question: What K-pop group released 'Dynamite'?
questions.explanation: Dynamite (2020) was BTS's first all-English single.

questions.question: What novel begins 'Call me Ishmael'?
incorrectAnswers: The Old Man and the Sea
questions.explanation: Herman Melville published Moby-Dick in 1851.

questions.question: Who sculpted 'The Thinker'?
questions.explanation: Rodin created The Thinker around 1880.

questions.question: What animated series features a talking sponge?
questions.explanation: SpongeBob premiered on Nickelodeon in 1999.

questions.question: Who wrote the 'Sherlock Holmes' stories?
questions.explanation: Doyle published the first Holmes story in 1887.

questions.question: What is the name of the hobbit played by Elijah Wood?
questions.explanation: Frodo carries the One Ring in the Lord of the Rings films.

questions.question: What opera features the 'Ride of the Valkyries'?
questions.explanation: Wagner composed Die Walküre as part of his Ring Cycle.

questions.question: What superhero is also known as the 'Man of Steel'?
questions.explanation: Superman first appeared in Action Comics #1 in 1938.

questions.question: Who directed '2001: A Space Odyssey'?
questions.explanation: Kubrick released the sci-fi masterpiece in 1968.
